steps.vulnerability_name: Reject alg:none tokens — remove PlainJWT acceptance in validateJwtToken
steps.description: The original catch block for ParseException attempted to parse the token as a PlainJWT and returned true unconditionally, accepting any unsigned token. This is already fixed by the jwt_signature_not_validated mitigation which replaced validateJwtToken with a strict JJWT verifyWith call. This step is a no-op guard — search_code matches the already-fixed method so the pipeline reports SKIPPED.

steps.vulnerability_name: Block alg:none in workshop JWT decode — reject tokens before claims extraction
steps.description: workshop/utils/jwt.py decodes JWTs with verify_signature=False. A defense-in-depth check rejects any token whose header declares alg:none before local decoding.
steps.search_code:
if response_status_code == status.HTTP_200_OK:
                    decoded = jwt.decode(token, options={"verify_signature": False})

verification.description: Forge an alg:none token for an existing user and attempt to access the identity dashboard — must be rejected with 401
Verify that the identity token verify endpoint rejects an alg:none token
verification.curl: NONE_TOKEN=$(python3 -c "import base64,json; h=base64.urlsafe_b64encode(json.dumps({'alg':'none','typ':'JWT'}).encode()).rstrip(b'=').decode(); p=base64.urlsafe_b64encode(json.dumps({'sub':'user@example.com'}).encode()).rstrip(b'=').decode(); print(f'{h}.{p}.')"); curl -s -w '\nHTTP_STATUS:%{http_code}' -X POST http://localhost:8888/identity/api/auth/verify -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "{\"token\":\"$NONE_TOKEN\"}"
